sn35.93:1.1: “It’s in dependence on a pair that consciousness comes into play.
sn35.93:1.2: And how does consciousness come into play in dependence on a pair?
sn35.93:1.3: In dependence on the eye & forms there arises eye-consciousness.
sn35.93:1.4: The eye is inconstant, changeable, of a nature to become otherwise.
sn35.93:1.5: Forms are inconstant, changeable, of a nature to become otherwise.
sn35.93:1.6: Thus this pair is both wavering & fluctuating—inconstant, changeable, of a nature to become otherwise.
sn35.93:1.7: “Eye-consciousness is inconstant, changeable, of a nature to become otherwise.
sn35.93:1.8: Whatever is the cause, the requisite condition, for the arising of eye-consciousness, that is inconstant, changeable, of a nature to become otherwise.
sn35.93:1.9: Having arisen in dependence on an inconstant factor, how could eye-consciousness be constant?
sn35.93:1.10: “The coming together, the meeting, the convergence of these three phenomena is eye-contact.
sn35.93:1.11: Whatever is the cause, the requisite condition, for the arising of eye-contact, that is inconstant, changeable, of a nature to become otherwise.
sn35.93:1.13: Having arisen in dependence on an inconstant factor, how could eye-contact be constant?
sn35.93:1.14: “Contacted, one feels. Contacted, one intends. Contacted, one perceives.
sn35.93:1.15: These phenomena are both wavering & fluctuating—inconstant, changeable, of a nature to become otherwise. This is how it’s in dependence on a pair that eye-consciousness comes into play.
sn35.93:2.1: “In dependence on the tongue & flavors there arises tongue-consciousness.…
sn35.93:3.1: “In dependence on the intellect & ideas there arises intellect-consciousness.
sn35.93:3.2: The intellect is inconstant, changeable, of a nature to become otherwise.
sn35.93:3.3: Ideas are inconstant, changeable, of a nature to become otherwise.
sn35.93:3.4: Thus this pair is both wavering & fluctuating—inconstant, changeable, of a nature to become otherwise.
sn35.93:3.5: “Intellect-consciousness is inconstant, changeable, of a nature to become otherwise.
sn35.93:3.6: Whatever is the cause, the requisite condition, for the arising of intellect-consciousness, that is inconstant, changeable, of a nature to become otherwise.
sn35.93:3.7: Having arisen in dependence on an inconstant factor, how could intellect-consciousness be constant?
sn35.93:3.8: “The coming together, the meeting, the convergence of these three phenomena is intellect-contact.
sn35.93:3.10: Whatever is the cause, the requisite condition, for the arising of intellect-contact, that is inconstant, changeable, of a nature to become otherwise.
sn35.93:3.11: Having arisen in dependence on an inconstant factor, how could intellect-contact be constant?
sn35.93:3.12: “Contacted, one feels. Contacted, one intends. Contacted, one perceives.
sn35.93:3.13: These phenomena are both wavering & fluctuating—inconstant, changeable, of a nature to become otherwise.
sn35.93:3.14: This is how it’s in dependence on a pair that intellect-consciousness comes into play.”
